title: Ginny and the Birth of Bonney
description: On pages 8–10, this part focuses on Unknown person #7, Bonney, Unknown person #8, Unknown person #9, Sabo, Ivankov as Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.
scene: Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Ooooh!:) You're quite the heavy drinker, Bonney-chan!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm sure you'll be just as much of a glutton as Ginny was!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: I'm still hungry...

dialogue.speaker: Bonney
dialogue.text: Daddy!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: KUMA?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: What in the world are you doing?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You've boarded up all of the Church's Windows!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Huff... huff!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: PANT... PAINT!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Close the door.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: TAKE A LOOK HERE, BELOW HER EYE...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Is that a blue stone?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Hey... isn't that...?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It looks just like what Ginny had at the end...!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I'll call a doctor!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Thank you!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hmm... it's caused by sunlight, you say? Are you sure? Why don't we try taking her outside?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Are you insane?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It doesn't seem to be white lead poisoning...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm sorry, I'm not familiar with this condition.

dialogue.speaker: Sabo
dialogue.text: You're quittingk the revolutionary army?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And Bonney's contracted the same disease her mother had?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I don't know if it's the right thing to do, but...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I have to stay by her side.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I understand. It's important to live without regrets.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We can use our contacts to reach out to doctors all over the world for her.

dialogue.speaker: Ivankov
dialogue.text: I owe you, Dragon.

summary: This part advances the Kuma and Bonney’s Tragedy through 28 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
